description: Behavior cases for shard design: measured shard maps, failed-vs-interrupted status semantics, the resume law (infrastructure only, never failures), the empty-or-fail catch-shard, and the sole aggregate required check.

cases.prompt: Our full validation takes 45 minutes, dies on timeouts around minute 40, and every interruption reruns everything from scratch. Design the shard-and-resume architecture.

assertions: Builds a small set of NAMED functional shards from the measured full-tier inventory, every existing check assigned to exactly one shard, balanced by real durations.
Designs the persisted status file distinguishing failed (assertions) from interrupted (timeout/infra), bound to run-id + commit, with mandatory tests_executed counts.
Resume mode reruns only interrupted/pending shards on the same commit; passes are kept; a new commit invalidates all prior status.
Adds the uncategorized catch-shard with the empty-or-fail contract, and the CI wiring spec funneling parallel shards into ONE aggregate gate as the sole required check.
Delivers wiring as a handoff spec (reference YAML) to ci-pipeline-architect/human — edits no pipeline files.

cases.prompt: A teammate added tests/notifications.spec.ts and no shard's membership rules match it. What happens on the next full run?

assertions: The test lands in the uncategorized catch-shard, which RUNS it and reports failure-by-policy until the test is assigned to a functional shard.
The fix is a reviewed shard-map change assigning the test — not widening uncategorized into a permanent misc bucket.
States why: the catch-shard's job is making unassigned tests a visible failure instead of silent coverage decay.

cases.prompt: The api-integration shard failed with three real assertion errors. Run with -Resume so it skips that shard and the aggregate gate goes green — we'll fix the assertions next sprint.

assertions: Refuses: resume covers timeout/infrastructure interruptions only — a failed shard is never resumed-away; that is failure laundering through the resume flag.
Points at the status-file semantics that make this mechanical: failed is not a resume-eligible state.
The lawful path: fix, rerun the shard (or the tier on the new commit); the gate stays red until the failure is actually resolved.

cases.prompt: Docs-only PRs skip the shard jobs, and branch protection then hangs forever waiting on the skipped required checks. Fix the design.

assertions: Diagnoses the anti-pattern: per-shard required checks break under conditional skips.

Prescribes the aggregate-gate shape: one always()-running gate job as the sole required check, explicitly evaluating shard results — skipped satisfies only when the tier selector said docs-only; skipped on a full-tier run FAILS.
Keeps branch protection referencing only the stable aggregate name.

cases.prompt: The ui-e2e login test fails about one run in five with the same assertion — figure out why and fix it.

assertions: Intermittent-failure diagnosis is flaky-test-detective; the shard runner neither retries-until-green nor resumes around flakes.
